How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-23 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing in 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day, surfacing edge from validated inter-screener combinations ranked by overlap score.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PANW leads the batch at a score of 567 - more than 2.5x the next name - combining CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and VOLUME_LEADER with no mixed signals, making it the clear first look. IREN at 164 is the only name with four screeners and the only one with CRYPTO_ETF exposure, adding a distinct risk profile. The score distribution is severely top-heavy: one outlier at 567, a thin middle cluster from AMAT (205) down through CDNS (146) and MS (124), then AVGO at the bottom of the list at 99. No pick matched a VALID combo today (all matched_combo null), so none of the statistically confirmed edges - SMART_FLOW times UNUSUAL_V3, three-plus VALID screener overlap, or end-of-month UNUSUAL_V3 - are engaged. PULLBACK appears in all ten names, meaning this is a pullback-in-trend batch rather than a breakout or options-flow-driven one, and a NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.28 offers no tailwind. Lead with PANW on score dominance alone, but hold conviction to medium given the absence of a validated combo trigger.

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Read
Top blow-off candidate today: AMAT (score 10, target ~$526.05 (DD20d -9.7%)).

9 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional options sweeps where next-day OI delta confirms opening rather than closing intent, filtered by EMA8/21 trend structure.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $CORZ $TLT $COIN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1CORZ ↑64 Watch$28.76 -1.37%BULLISH SWEEPCALL33$101KOPENING130667STRONG33
2TLT ↑55 Watch$87.14 +1.08%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNEUTRAL0100STRONG1010
3COIN ↑50 Watch$158.17 -0.01%BULLISH SWEEPCALL182.5$225KOPENING145576BROKEN44
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking is statistically void, with only 5 resolved trades out of 282 and a verdict of WATCH, so the reported 100% win rate and 200% average return cannot be used to confirm or challenge the backtest Sharpe of 2.39 and 68.1% win rate - the monthly cohorts similarly show 100% win rates across April and May which reflects near-zero resolution, not genuine performance.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 285 signals captured · 5 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +4.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 282 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin: two signals, both Watch tier, both LONG via bullish sweeps. TLT scores 55 and leads the board, flagging potential bond demand. COIN sits at 50, the weaker of the two. Score distribution is compressed and bottom-heavy, with a median of 50, a ceiling of 55, and nothing above 65, making this one of the softer batches the screen can produce. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.28 does not add a tailwind or a headwind, leaving both setups reliant on the sweep signal alone rather than macro confirmation. If forced to prioritize, TLT first at low conviction, given its marginal edge in score and the current fixed income bid visible in rate markets, but neither name clears a high-conviction threshold today.

🧠 Ranks premium-selling candidates by IV30-HV30 spread with Sinclair sector bias and value filters, exploiting the structural tendency of implied vol to exceed realized vol.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record of 57.2% WR and +4.2% avg return against SPY +1.0% confirms the backtest Sharpe of 2.34 is not collapsing in production, and May's cohort (n=512, WR 57.2%, avg +4.5%) holds the same line as April (WR 53.3%, avg +6.7%), making the +3.2-point SPY spread the key anchor that keeps this screener on the KEEP list.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 500 closed · WR TB 36% · 23% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NEE tops the batch at 78, with PCG and EXC both at 77, three utilities that land precisely in Sinclair's +31% sector-bias sweet spot for variance premium harvesting. CL at 76 and KMB at 74 add staples depth, keeping the entire top eight in low-beta, high-dividend territory. Score distribution is compressed: max 78, median 66, zero names above 80, and 36 of 50 signals above 65 - a mid-conviction, tightly clustered batch with no outlier conviction. Macro NEUTRAL at 54.28 is permissive rather than a tailwind, and the defensive sector tilt fits a sideways tape better than a risk-on one. Start with NEE first at half-size given top score and cleanest sector alignment, no pattern data to sharpen entry timing.

🧠 Captures tickers where implied vol sits in the middle tertile versus realized vol, isolating names where options are priced coherently rather than distorted by disbelief or a live catalyst.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PATH at 100 and RDDT at 99 lead a batch that is genuinely top-heavy: 13 of 30 names score 80 or above, median sits at 75, and 22 of 30 clear 65, so the distribution clusters high rather than sprawling across the range. UPST at 97 adds a third fintech-adjacent name with very strong tier confirmation, which raises the question of whether this is a real factor signal or a sector cluster worth monitoring. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.28 is the natural habitat for this screener, since coherent vol pricing tends to break down in trending regimes, so the fit here is solid. Take PATH first on highest raw score with moderate conviction, no patterns are reported so size accordingly and let the vol surface confirm before adding.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is fully saturated: all 50 signals print 100, so the screener provides no internal ranking today and every name sits at the ceiling. AME and CDNS are the most defensible candidates on sector quality (industrial compounding and EDA software respectively), but that preference comes from outside this dataset because pattern fields are null across the entire batch. BFLY and ACHV carry materially thinner liquidity. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.28) neither amplifies nor undermines the contrarian hedge-read thesis. With zero score differentiation and no pattern data, conviction is moderate - AME first, sized small until the tape confirms.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record at 64.5% win rate and +2.1% average versus SPY's -0.61% confirms the backtest's 65.0% win rate almost exactly - but the monthly cohort trend is a soft warning, with May dropping to 50.6% win rate at +0.99% average against March's 67.0% and +4.9%, a clear two-month deceleration that argues for tighter position sizing until the cohort numbers stabilize.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

INV leads at a perfect score of 100, Very strong tier, LAUNCH pattern - that is the highest-conviction name in today's batch. LGN at 95 is the clean secondary, with ENS, QCOM, RAMP, RELY, and RMAX all clustering at 90, all Very strong LAUNCH signals. The score distribution is unusually compressed at the top: all 50 signals clear the 80 threshold and the median sits at 81, which means today's batch is either genuinely strong or the screen is crowded with names sitting just above the floor rather than well above it. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.28, offering no directional tailwind but not killing the thesis either, as pullback reversals function in range-bound tape provided the underlying trends remain intact. Lead with INV at full half-Kelly sizing, LGN as the secondary at reduced size, and treat the 90-score cluster as a group requiring further fundamental filtering before committing.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Six names tie at the top score of 94 - MNDY, NIO, ZS, RR, SLDP, and UPWK - all rated Very strong, with LI and SYM close behind at 92. No pattern data is available for any of the 28 names, which removes one differentiating layer that would otherwise help rank within the basket. The score distribution is unusually compressed: all 28 signals sit between 91 and 94 with a median of 91, so the screen offers no real gradient today and the whole batch looks homogeneous. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.28, neither a tailwind nor a direct headwind for a contrarian mean-reversion basket, but it does not add conviction. If forced to size first, MNDY and ZS offer deeper and more liquid options markets for LEAPS construction, but at identical scores and zero pattern differentiation, treat this as a thin, clustered batch where basket sizing at 1-2% per name is the only defensible approach.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax by combining VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to identify peak-fear sell-vol setups.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑58 Watch$737.00 +0.47%SELL VOL1.01
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows a 71% win rate and a +1.93% avg 20-day return versus SPY at +1.91%, a spread of essentially zero points, so the screen is keeping pace with the index but generating no net alpha, and the Signal tier is doing the real work at 85.7% and +2.74% while April's four-trade cohort going 100% at +5.4% avg flatters the overall record more than May's 68.75% and +1.28% deserves.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Detects unusual options flow (volume 5x 20-day average, $50K+ premium, Vol/OI above 5) in calm, non-extended stocks to filter late-chasing signals.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Only 25 of 620 signals have resolved in the 90-day window (4% completion), so the 100% win rate and +188.6% average are statistically hollow, and the backtest's own p_bonferroni of 0.097 with rigorous=false and no DSR keeps this screener firmly MARGINAL despite the KEEP label.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

WULF prints the highest score at 92 (Very strong) but immediately raises a flag: the system tags direction LONG on a BEARISH SWEEP pattern, which is an internal contradiction that demands confirmation before touching it. SOXX at 79 and PTON at 71 are the cleaner reads, both Signal-tier with confirmed bullish sweeps and no directional ambiguity. The 14-signal batch is middle-heavy, median 60, only one name above 80 and five above 65, so the distribution is thin at the top and not particularly high-conviction as a group. NEUTRAL macro (54.28) provides no tailwind for the predominantly bullish flow, which means the edge here is stock-specific, not regime-driven. First preference is SOXX at moderate conviction - clean bullish sweep, sector-level liquidity, and no directional conflict.

🧠 Detects sector momentum phase shifts by scoring 11 GICS ETFs on strength, acceleration, and RS crossover versus SPY into eight discrete categories.
Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLU at 95 (Very Strong) is the standout, utilities leading the entire sector universe in a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.28) - a clear defensive rotation signal, not a risk-on one. XLE and XLC both print 80 (Strong), offering the only cyclical exposure in the top tier, though both carry null pattern data, which limits conviction on entry timing. The score distribution is sharply bimodal: five names at 80 or above and five clustered between 50 and 55, with nothing in the 60-79 range, suggesting the tape is splitting fast between leaders and laggards rather than lifting broadly. The defensive lean fits the NEUTRAL regime read. XLU is the first name at high conviction; XLE second given it adds cyclical diversification, but position sizing should reflect the MARGINAL backtest and the absence of any live tracking record.

🧠 Identifies deep-oversold equities (down 40%-plus from 52-week high) showing capitulation volume and a reversal candle, betting on mean reversion within 15 days.
Construction

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking contradicts the backtest optimism: 90-day resolved trades show a 44% win rate against a 63% backtest figure, with the 'Strong' tier - the most actionable below 'Very Strong' - delivering a deeply negative avg of -8.7%, and only the small 'Signal' tier (n=175, 55% WR, +8.2% avg) saving the aggregate; the net read is WATCH, not a green light.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 498 resolved at +20d · WR 44% · avg +1.4% · vs SPY +0.3%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 491 closed · WR TB 37% · 10% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days, buying only smooth uptrends above MA100 when SPY clears MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects post-earnings drift using EPS surprise size, gap magnitude, gap-hold price action, and analyst revision activity as the continuation signal.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BBCP $BKE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BBCP ↑90●●● Very strong$11.06 -0.18%500.0%437.6%
2BKE ↑70● Signal$44.41 +0.58%24.7%4-3.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape (63% WR, avg +4.9% on 495 resolved trades, beating SPY by 4.7 points) confirms the screener carries real edge at short windows and is directionally consistent with May 2026's 61.5% WR on 621 signals - the key number is that 4.7-point SPY spread, which is the only reason the MARGINAL backtest verdict still warrants a KEEP.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more intermarket pairs break 2-sigma correlation, exploiting mean reversion over 60 to 120 days.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (61% WR, +5.2% avg, +5.4 pts versus SPY at -0.1%) supports a KEEP, but the tier inversion is the key number: 'Very strong' names resolve at only 46.4% WR versus 'Signal' at 76.9%, directly contradicting the assumption that a higher assigned tier means better forward returns.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is completely collapsed: all 50 signals score 100, median 100, zero spread. That uniform ceiling offers no internal ranking, so the top_picks list is essentially arbitrary ordering. Among the names, CHD and CMS are the most coherent candidates - defensive consumer staples and utility crossing up into a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.28), where low-beta trend resumptions tend to hold better than cyclicals. DVN is the contrarian energy name worth watching if crude stabilizes. The critical problem: every pick today lands in the 'Very strong' tier, which live tracking shows is the worst-performing cohort at 46.4% WR and +3.3% avg versus 'Signal' tier's 76.9% WR and +10.4% avg. CHD first, low conviction, size accordingly.

🧠 Identifies stocks with high short interest, rising borrow cost, and a technical breakout that can force short covering.
Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape supports running this screener: 63% win rate, avg +7.4% over 20 days versus SPY at +0.4%, a spread of 7.0 points that is hard to dismiss, though the tier data complicates the picture - Watch-tier names (66.7% WR, avg +9.6%) outperform Very Strong (50% WR, avg +2.1%), suggesting score rank does not cleanly predict outcome, a pattern the May 2026 cohort (WR 56.5%, avg +4.0%) supports while April 2026 (WR 44.4%, avg -0.7%) serves as a reminder that regime timing remains the key variable.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BEAM and DDD both print at 90, the top score in today's batch, with PRIMED patterns - two very different businesses (biotech and 3D printing) sharing the same mechanical setup of crowded short position meeting price momentum. APGE, BHVN, CAKE, and CBRL all sit at 85, giving the top of the board real density. Score distribution is top-heavy but not thin: 12 names at or above 80, 48 of 50 at or above 65, with median at 70 - the batch is populated and the signal is not diluted across junk. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.28, which neither turbochages nor kills a squeeze thesis, though binary, event-driven names like BEAM and APGE (both appear biotech-adjacent) carry idiosyncratic risk that macro regime cannot price. First preference is BEAM at Very Strong, watched before DDD given biotech short squeezes historically accelerate faster once they start, but conviction is moderate given the anecdotal-only backtest.

🧠 Ranks Russell-1000 stocks by relative volume across three lookback windows multiplied by price momentum across four horizons, filtering for upward 20-day price trend.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 The live tape flatly contradicts any edge: 44% win rate and -2.6% avg 20-day return versus SPY at -1.6% gives a -1.0 point alpha, the options book is worse at 30.8% win rate, and the backtest itself is flagged survivor-biased and unvalidated, making the KILL verdict the only honest read here.

HOOD, MU, and WDC sit at score 100 Very strong, with MRVL and SNDK just behind at 99 in the same tier, making semis and fintech the dominant themes in today's batch. The score distribution is entirely compressed: all 50 signals score 80 or above, median 95, which means the screener is offering zero rank differentiation today and every name looks equally 'loud.' Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54, neither a tailwind nor a headwind for momentum. Critically, live tracking shows the Very strong tier is the worst performer in the book, averaging -5.83% at 20 days versus +0.83% for the Strong tier, so chasing the top scores is actively penalized in recent history. If forced to act, prefer nothing from this batch, or at most a very small, speculative position in MU given sector fundamentals, with minimal conviction.

🧠 Detects institutional options block trades over $200K, short-dated OTM on quiet stocks, confirming new positions via next-day OI delta.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape - 61% WR and avg +4.4% on 454 resolved trades beating SPY by 3.5 points - confirms the observational edge holds in real conditions, and the May and April 2026 monthly cohorts running 54-55% WR with positive average returns show no degradation, though the 41% options-basket win rate is the key number to watch, since it suggests the underlying stock move rather than holding options to expiry is where the return actually lives.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RSPF scores 91, the sole Very Strong name in today's 37-signal batch, and the premium weight plus OI confirmation behind that score makes it the clear lead with no close competitor. SMQ, KW, and VSCO cluster at 81-82 in Strong tier, all pointing long, though pattern data is null across every pick today, which strips out one layer of context we would normally lean on. The score distribution is thin at the top: only 4 names clear 80, the median sits at 63, and roughly half the batch is background noise below the Signal threshold. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.28, which neither amplifies nor suppresses a flow-based screen like this one. Take RSPF first at high conviction given the 9-point gap to the next name; size the SMQ-KW-VSCO cluster at half weight until pattern confirmation shows up.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Catches post-earnings drift by waiting for a weekly red-candle pullback within four weeks of the gap, then buying the weekly reclaim above the prior week's high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5

Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Three signals today, a thin batch with no pattern data attached to any name. ACA prints a perfect 100 in the Very Strong tier and is the lead candidate on score alone. AUPH at 80 sits in the Strong tier, which carries the best live performance of any cohort in the 90-day window, averaging +27.3% on 10 resolved trades at 70% win rate, so the tier-level read on AUPH is constructive despite the absence of pattern detail. ALT scores 77 in the Signal tier, thin historical sample, lower conviction. The distribution is top-heavy with two of three names at 80 or above and a median of 80, which is encouraging for a three-name day. NEUTRAL macro at 54.28 neither tailwinds nor fights the setup. ACA first at high conviction given the max score, with AUPH as a secondary watch given the Strong-tier track record.

Data confidence STARTER prod-cabled KVO+FORCE combo, paper KEPT WR 67% vs SKIPPED 46% over 60d; no formal Bonferroni verdict yet Full methodology →
🧠 Removes noisy SMART_FLOW entries where KVO or FORCE INDEX are flipping within three days, keeping only volume-confirmed clean institutional flow signals.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $TLT $COIN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑55 Watch$87.14 +1.08%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNEUTRAL0100STRONG1010
2COIN ↑50 Watch$158.17 -0.01%BULLISH SWEEPCALL182.5$225KOPENING145576BROKEN44
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Two signals, both Watch-tier, nothing scoring above 55 - this is a weak batch. TLT leads at 55 with a BULLISH SWEEP pattern; a long-bond sweep in a NEUTRAL macro (54.28) is coherent but carries no urgency. COIN scores 50, also a BULLISH SWEEP, sitting at the floor of today's distribution and dependent on risk appetite the macro does not strongly support. The score distribution is extremely thin - two names, median 50, zero signals at 65 or above. If forced to act, TLT is the first look at low conviction only; nothing here warrants pressing.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional rejection at Market Profile value-area extremes via tail bars at least 1.8x ATR, exploiting mean reversion from price locations below 30 percent or above 70 percent of value.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $STRC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1STRC ↑100●●● Very strong$86.10 -1.39%LOW2.6126.4%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape shows 100 percent win rate and plus 7.31 percent average 20-day return against SPY at plus 1.16 percent across the two resolved trades, which directionally supports the backtest filter result of plus 3.18 percent at 10 days, but the WATCH verdict is the honest frame here because two resolved trades is nowhere near the 20 needed to trust that number.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms the screener has real edge at the top tiers - Strong names average +18.8% at 65% WR and Very Strong names +13.9% at 59% WR over 90 days versus SPY at +1.0% - but that edge does not survive into Watch tier, which the monthly cohorts corroborate: April and May cohorts improved sharply (avg +12.7% and +7.7%) as more high-tier names entered, while March's near-flat +1.2% came when the pipeline was thin, suggesting the 53.4% headline WR is almost entirely carried by the top two tiers, not the screener broadly.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day KEEP verdict (54.2% WR, avg +2.7%, beating SPY +1.6% by 1.1 points) superficially contradicts the REJECTED backtest, but the Strong-tier avg of -20.8% is the single number that explains the discrepancy and disqualifies today's highest-scoring names, while monthly cohorts from March through May show no deterioration in Signal-tier performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMWB (85, Strong, TRIGGER) and AMC (80, Strong, TRIGGER) carry the two highest scores today, but the Strong tier is a historical trap: 20% win rate and avg return of -20.8% across 10 resolved names means these signals have reliably lost money on the short side, and that is exactly where today's top two names sit. The four Signal-tier names at 75 - BFLY, BTQ, NBIS, and WDC - are in SOW ZONE and belong to the tier that actually produces results (59.3% WR, avg +8.98%). Score distribution is middle-clustered, with 37 of 50 signals at or above 65 and only 2 above 80, so this is not a top-heavy day with a clean edge at the top. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.28, providing no directional wind for short positioning. First look goes to BFLY or BTQ from the Signal tier, bypassing the Strong-tier TRIGGER names entirely.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is informationally hollow. All 50 signals score between 99 and 100, every name sits in 'Very strong' tier, and no pattern data is attached to any pick - that is a score distribution so compressed it carries no rank-order signal. Of the tickers surfaced, RIOT and NBIS stand out only because they operate in sectors with recent price momentum (crypto infrastructure and AI compute), but the model provides zero pattern context to differentiate them from QBTX, ELTX, PPLT, PTIR, OSCR, or PALL. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.28, which neither supports nor kills a momentum tilt. Conviction here is low: do not act on any name from this batch until the model produces a score distribution with real dispersion.

🧠 Flags deeply oversold weekly RSI stocks and times long entries via daily RSI crossover, candlestick reversal confirmation, and zone scoring.
Construction

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms the KILL verdict: a 72% win rate looks superficially decent but the avg +4.6% trails SPY by 2.5 points, and monthly cohorts show a brutal deterioration in avg return from +6.6% in March to +0.4% in May, the key number that makes the backtest irrelevant.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DDL leads at 70 (Signal, SETUP) and DKL sits at 66 (Signal, SETUP) - those are the only two names clearing the 65 threshold out of 26 signals, making this a thin batch with a compressed score distribution (median 53, zero names above 80). BORR at 63 is the next name down but slips into Watch territory, and the remaining names cluster between 58 and 61 with no distinguishing separation. NEUTRAL macro at 54.28 neither supports nor resists a contrarian bounce play, which is about as benign as this regime gets. Take DDL first given the top score and Signal tier, but size it small - the screener carries a doctrine-rejected backtest and the batch quality is weak.

🧠 Detects unusual flow intensity and OI delta spikes on crypto-linked ETFs, using spot correlation to infer directional positioning pressure in mining and holding equities.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The headline 90-day verdict of KEEP at 67% win rate and +2.0% average is completely inverted at the tier level - Watch (n=58) drives the entire result at 75.9% WR and +5.3% avg, while Signal and Strong tiers, which represent today's best-scoring names, have combined to average roughly -18% per resolved trade, contradicting the backtest and undermining any confidence in the screener's scoring gradient.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HUT at 85 and IREN at 80 lead the batch, both tagged Strong-tier TRIGGER longs, but the tier data is a direct warning: Strong-tier signals across the trailing 90 days produced a 0% win rate and averaged -13.2% over 20 days on a single resolved trade. CLSK and MARA sit at 70 in the Signal tier, which resolves at 12.5% win rate and -20.1% average. The only tier with a positive live record is Watch, where CORZ and RIOT sit at 60. Score distribution is thin at 6 signals, top-heavy with 2 names above 80 and median at 70, and a NEUTRAL macro regime at 54 adds no tailwind to a batch that is already leaning on the two historically worst-performing tiers. If forced to act, the sole actionable preference is CORZ or RIOT at low size, as Watch-tier is the only tier with a positive live return, but conviction is low given the backtest rejection and noisy crypto tape.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ARKG leads today's batch at 74, the only name clearing the Signal tier with meaningful separation from the pack, though genomics has been a momentum graveyard and no pattern data supports the setup. SMIN and IFRA both score 67, a tie that reads more like noise than conviction given the absence of any pattern context. The score distribution is compressed and weak: median 53, nothing above 74, zero names above 80, and only four clearing 65 out of 50 signals. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.28 neither blesses nor blocks theme exposure, but it offers no tailwind for high-beta thematic ETFs. If forced to act, ARKG first at low conviction, sized small until pattern confirmation emerges.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and LNBIX both score 100 and lead the batch, though pattern data is null for every name so the quality of each buy, cluster size, and dollar magnitude cannot be verified here. HOOD at 98 is the only broadly liquid name and therefore the most actionable from an execution standpoint. The score distribution is extreme: all 50 signals score at or above 80, median 94, which signals the filter is compressing everything to the top and providing almost no differentiation within the batch. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.28 does not actively support or kill insider-buy signals, but it removes any tailwind. Given the liquidity constraint and recognizable C-suite signal history, HOOD is the first name to look at, low conviction until dollar size and executive rank are confirmed.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names hit a perfect 100 today, all Very strong, all LONG: BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM. The presence of SRTY, an inverse small-cap ETF, alongside dividend defensive HDV and volatile consumer name CELH creates no coherent sector thesis, and no patterns are populated for any pick, so selection is score-only with no structural context. Score distribution is top-heavy: 12 of 50 signals reach 80 or above, median sits at 70, and 41 of 50 clear 65. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.28, adding no directional edge. Historical tracking shows Watch-tier names averaging 9.2% vs Very strong at 3.4%, meaning the perfect-100 cohort is actually the weaker performer in this screener, so conviction across this batch is low and no name earns a first call until patterns are populated.

🧠 Aggregates quarterly SEC 13F filings from 20 top hedge funds, scoring new and increased positions to surface names with the broadest institutional conviction.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names in 'Very strong' territory, meaning multiple funds initiated or heavily added to both within the same filing period. AVGO at 79 sits just below the top tier with broad incremental adds rather than a single large new entry. The score distribution is top-heavy: a median of 55 with only 2 names above 80 out of 50 signals tells you most names picked up one or two modest increases, nothing more. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.28 offers no directional tailwind, which is tolerable for a conviction-following strategy but means the signal carries the full weight. First look is ASML given its score, but remember every name here is 60 to 90 days stale by construction, so price has already moved off the filing date. Treat this as a watchlist, not an entry trigger, and layer a technical or momentum filter before pulling the trigger.

🧠 Ranks US equities by EV/EBIT ascending, scoring 100 for the cheapest operating earner, betting extreme enterprise cheapness mean-reverts.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ tops the batch at a perfect 100 with a Very Strong tier, making it the most extreme value read of the day; WRD (97) and KROS (93) follow closely, both Very Strong, though none of the three carry a pattern signal to add technical confirmation. GPC at 90 rounds out the four Very Strong names and is the most recognizable business of the group. The score distribution skews top-heavy: 7 of 16 signals print above 80 and 11 above 65, with a median of 73, so this is not a thin or scattered batch. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.28, which offers no tailwind for a deep-value rotation but is not an active headwind either. No backtest exists yet, so position sizing must be treated as exploratory; if forced to pick one, BZ at 100 goes first, at low conviction, sized small.

🧠 Detects coordinated insider conviction via SEC Form 4 cluster buys, weighting C-suite rank, dollar size, and recency into a single score.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape is the strongest thing going for this screener: 61% win rate, avg +3.8% at 20 days versus SPY's +1.4%, a +2.4-point edge across 308 resolved trades, and the May 2026 cohort (WR 65.5%, avg +4.3%) confirms the April softness (WR 47.1%, avg +2.0%) was a temporary dip rather than deterioration, though no rigorous backtest exists yet so those live numbers carry full model risk.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is flat: all 16 signals sit at a score of 60, the screener's floor, with zero names clearing 65 and no pattern tags on any ticker. Among the names, ADSK and FISV stand out only because they are large-cap, liquid names where insider clusters carry more signal-to-noise than micro-caps like BRID or GUTS at the same score. BORR is worth a second look given offshore driller cycle positioning, but the score gives no differentiation. The distribution is fully compressed, median equals max at 60, which typically means the cluster criteria were just barely met across the board. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.28), so there is no regime tailwind to rescue a weak batch. Conviction is low across the board; if forced to act, ADSK is the first name given liquidity and institutional legibility, but sizing should be minimal until a higher-scoring day refreshes the list.

🧠 Identifies individual stocks lagging their sector move within LATE_CHASE or RIDING sectors, scoring by sector relative strength times stock underperformance.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bull traps where price breaks above value area high then closes back inside, signaling trapped longs and a short entry.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AAXJ $ARTY $ASX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AAXJ ↓75● Signal$117.70 +0.84%BULL_TRAP118.4297.83
2ARTY ↓70● Signal$75.00 +0.78%BULL_TRAP75.8371.57
3ASX ↓70● Signal$41.09 +3.31%BULL_TRAP41.3435.16
4ADI ↓65● Signal$409.63 +0.58%BULL_TRAP431.49416.05
5AIRR ↓65● Signal$130.50 +0.76%BULL_TRAP130.81124.76
6AIS ↓65● Signal$81.36 +0.96%BULL_TRAP82.4280.90
7ANET ↓65● Signal$163.74 +0.95%BULL_TRAP167.51144.39
8ARW ↓65● Signal$224.35 -0.48%BULL_TRAP230.42189.83
9ASHR ↓65● Signal$35.89 -0.47%BULL_TRAP36.3634.81
10ALAB ↓60 Watch$417.92 +5.26%BULL_TRAP416.67340.37
11AME ↓60 Watch$234.30 +0.09%BULL_TRAP238.90232.78
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Two resolved trades is noise, not data, but both lost money on the short side at -4.77% average against a SPY baseline of -0.31%, which contradicts any short edge and leaves the verdict firmly at WATCH.

IONQ leads at 85 with a Strong bull tag, making it the top priority - quantum computing names have been sensitive to positioning shifts and this is the highest-conviction print in the batch. SOXX and TLT both score 80 Strong bull, an unusual pairing that suggests money is simultaneously chasing semis and duration, which fits a soft-landing narrative but creates internal tension in the batch. WULF at 70 is the lone bear signal, a Signal-tier short on a bitcoin miner that reads as a fade of recent crypto-adjacent momentum. Score distribution is tight and top-heavy: all 5 names clear 65, 3 clear 80, median 80, with no weak stragglers dragging down the cohort. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.28, which neither accelerates nor kills these setups but argues against sizing aggressively. First priority is IONQ bull at moderate conviction, with WULF bear as a secondary paired trade if the broader risk tape softens.

🧠 Flags retail-driven melt-ups via call volume spikes, Reddit surges, and parabolic price without earnings catalysts, then shorts the reversal.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $ASND $BXP
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1ASND ↓100●●● Very strong$241.65 +1.28%FOMO1.130.8%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where 30-day implied vol outruns 20-day realized vol by 1.5 sigma, surfacing candidates for short-premium options trades.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $CMCSA $NFLX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1CMCSA ↓63 Watch$23.03 +1.01%40%25%1.59FEAR
2NFLX ↓61 Watch$73.13 +0.43%44%29%1.53FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Put/call 25-delta skew spike vs 90-day baseline flags unusual hedging demand as a contrarian signal when the underlying price is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $ORCL $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1ORCL ↑72● Signal$164.64 -0.31%54%50-0.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
2AVGO ↑64 Watch$385.92 +1.52%48%62-0.02BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (62% WR, avg +12.4%, beating SPY by +10.5 points) confirms a KEEP verdict, but the key number is the tier inversion - Watch names average +19.6% while Very strong names average -1.02%, which directly contradicts the idea that higher scores here carry higher edge and undercuts today's headline GOOGL signal.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects false breakouts below VAL or above VAH that reverse the next day, trapping shorts or longs on the wrong side of the value area.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AZN $ACGL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AZN ↑60 Watch$183.86 +1.57%BEAR_TRAP178.64184.083.78
2ACGL ↑55 Watch$93.89 +0.20%BEAR_TRAP92.5694.251.77
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The resolved-trade headline of 85.71% win rate and +16.47% avg vs SPY's 0.52% looks compelling, but with only 7 resolved trades out of 72 and a time-based win rate of just 39.29% across 28 timed-out trades, the data is too thin and internally inconsistent to treat as confirmation of any edge.

🧠 Detects extreme bearish IV skew (put IV above 90th percentile vs calls), flagging unusual institutional put demand as a potential contrarian long signal.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$385.92 +1.52%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects pre-earnings straddles where implied vol trades below Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, flagging potential vol underpricing.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1MU ↓50 Watch$1074.21 +2.13%REVERSAL1DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak: one name, MU at score 50 (Watch tier), with no pattern data attached and a SHORT direction that carries no backtest support yet. #TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓86●● Strong$23.63 -1.01%56%257%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)

Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities using Altman Z-score distress signals to surface financial stress or recovery setups.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is empty. One signal fired and it scored zero, meaning nothing cleared any meaningful threshold. There are no top picks to evaluate. The score distribution is as thin as it gets: n_signals of 1, max of 0, median of 0, zero names above 65. Macro sits at NEUTRAL with a 54.28 score, which is a permissive backdrop, but a permissive tape cannot rescue a screener with nothing in it. No actionable name today.

🧠 STFR screens for a specific setup defined in its source script; no construction detail is documented here.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA (Volume Spread Analysis) detects institutional accumulation or distribution by comparing price spread to volume, flagging supply/demand imbalances before directional moves.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Pass on this screener today.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
